submodule"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-12-05T11:20:00Z","updated_at":"2024-12-06T05:06:35Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the feature

#7790 Introduced fsspec as a dependency primarily for s3 and other remote file system support. We can however extend fsspec to be our *only* interface for filesystem operations (i.e. instead of pathlib) and and file IO which should remove some duplication.

### Proposed solution

This requires a few changes in a number of places.

Firstly, `io._file_tools` should support being passed an [`fsspec.OpenFile`](https://filesystem-spec.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#fsspec.core.OpenFile) object rather than just a path (if passed a path it should convert to an `OpenFile`.

Secondly we should convert all http[s] handling in io and both factories to use fsspec, the only confusion here is how we use the cache (in Map factory, but not TimeSeries apparently). We might want to either drop this completely or use fsspec's caching or somehow make our cache fsspec aware.

Combine all handling for paths, http[s] urls and other URIs into one fsspec block and ensure that we don't cast back to a path / URI anywhere when opening files (we carry the fsspec objects through).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7900/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7900/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/comments/2522154843","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7900#issuecomment-2522154843","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7900","id":2522154843,"node_id":"IC_kwDOACEKh86WVQdb","user":{"login":"raghav20232023","id":153320363,"node_id":"U_kgDOCSN7qw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/153320363?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/raghav20232023","html_url":"https://github.com/raghav20232023","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/raghav20232023/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/raghav20232023/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/raghav20232023/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/raghav20232023/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/raghav20232023/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/raghav20232023/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/raghav20232023/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/raghav20232023/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/raghav20232023/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-06T05:06:34Z","updated_at":"2024-12-06T05:06:34Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"@Cadair I would like to work on this feature request. > 
> What about the failing tests? Are these something that need to be addressed?

Yes. I believe they are failing because of how s3:// files are being handled. Could be an issue because s3fs is not installed. 
Could be the fact that these are relative paths and fsspec requires them to be prefixed:

see documentation:
fsspec.open(urlpath, mode='rb' ...)

    Given a path or paths, return one OpenFile object.

    Parameters:

        urlpath: string or list

            **Absolute or relative filepath. Prefix with a protocol like s3:// to read from alternative filesystems. Should not include glob character(s).**
            
            reference : https://filesystem-spec.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#fsspec.open","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/comments/2565295491/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

Provide a general description of the issue or problem.

Currently in our `.ruff.toml` file we ignore the PT011 rule:

https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/blob/b656375f01eb2d2eceb0b1619c2a2e7851baa548/.ruff.toml#L28

This issue is to address all instances of this lint in the code and remove the ignore.

The Ruff error [PT011](https://docs.astral.sh/ruff/rules/pytest-raises-too-broad/) is triggered when in a test [`pytest.raises`](https://docs.pytest.org/en/4.6.x/reference.html#pytest-raises) is used without a `match=` keyword argument.

For example:

```python
def test_thing():
    pytest.raises(ValueError):
        thing()
```

This should be:

```python
def test_thing():
    pytest.raises(ValueError, match="thing should be called with an argument"):
        thing()
```

The `match=` keyword argument takes any regex which matches the error raised. When writing a match string it is best to pick either all or part of the error message which is unique and long enough to make sure the error being raised is specific to the code under test. If the error message is very long (>80 characters) it's best to not include the whole string. In some cases when writing a match string you might need to escape special characters or pick a substring without them.

To address this issue the following steps should be performed:

1. Remove the line from .ruff.toml
2. run `tox -e codestyle` which will give you a list of errors
3. For each error, you need to go through and add a `match=` kwarg specific to that error being raised in that test. One approach to this is to add a match kwarg which will not match i.e. `match="!!"` which will cause the test to fail when run with `tox -e py312` and the test fail will show you the error that was raised and you can pick a suitable string to put in that match kwarg.
4. Ensure that `tox -e codestyle` passes with no errors once all issues are addressed.
5. Provide a general description of the issue or problem.

Currently in our `.ruff.toml` file we ignore the PT011 rule:

https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/blob/b656375f01eb2d2eceb0b1619c2a2e7851baa548/.ruff.toml#L28

This issue is to address all instances of this lint in the code and remove the ignore.

The Ruff error [PT011](https://docs.astral.sh/ruff/rules/pytest-raises-too-broad/) is triggered when in a test [`pytest.raises`](https://docs.pytest.org/en/4.6.x/reference.html#pytest-raises) is used without a `match=` keyword argument.

For example:

```python
def test_thing():
    pytest.raises(ValueError):
        thing()
```

This should be:

```python
def test_thing():
    pytest.raises(ValueError, match="thing should be called with an argument"):
        thing()
```

The `match=` keyword argument takes any regex which matches the error raised. When writing a match string it is best to pick either all or part of the error message which is unique and long enough to make sure the error being raised is specific to the code under test. If the error message is very long (>80 characters) it's best to not include the whole string. In some cases when writing a match string you might need to escape special characters or pick a substring without them.

To address this issue the following steps should be performed:

1. Remove the line from .ruff.toml
2. run `tox -e codestyle` which will give you a list of errors
3. For each error, you need to go through and add a `match=` kwarg specific to that error being raised in that test. One approach to this is to add a match kwarg which will not match i.e. `match="!!"` which will cause the test to fail when run with `tox -e py312` and the test fail will show you the error that was raised and you can pick a suitable string to put in that match kwarg.
4. Ensure that `tox -e codestyle` passes with no errors once all issues are addressed.
5.
